meaning: adj.第三的,第三位的,第三世纪的n.[画]第三色,第三修道会会员,[地]第三纪
examples: He must have come to know those philosophers through secondary or tertiary sources.
The Tertiary Period was separated into division of time that were equal in length.
This change in plankton found in marine deposits is what marks the boundary between the Cretaceous and the Tertiary.
We also have a similar system in tertiary or higher (or even college) education.
The rise in application interest, the subject of the current research, is probably the tertiary reason.
